Technical Glossary | 2timeweb

Master fundamental technology and SaaS concepts with clear definitions, practical examples, and resources to deepen your knowledge.

SaaS

Software as a Service is a cloud-based software distribution model where applications are hosted remotely and accessed via the internet, eliminating local installation.

Practical Example

“Tools like Google Workspace, Slack, and Notion are SaaS examples. You pay a monthly subscription and use the software directly in your browser.”

Learn More

  • How to Scale a SaaS from Zero
  • SaaS Monetization: Subscription Models

Micro-SaaS

Micro-SaaS are small, niche-focused software products that solve very specific problems for a targeted market, operated by lean teams with minimal overhead.

Practical Example

“A browser extension that automates LinkedIn follow-ups for recruiters, or a dashboard tracking specific e-commerce metrics.”

Learn More

  • Building a Profitable Micro-SaaS
  • Lean SaaS Idea Validation

AI Automation

AI Automation combines workflow automation with artificial intelligence capabilities (NLP, computer vision, generative models) to make intelligent decisions and adapt over time.

Practical Example

“A system that automatically analyzes support tickets, classifies priority using NLP, and suggests responses based on historical data.”

Learn More

  • Integrating AI into Workflows
  • Top AI Automation Tools in 2025

CRM

Customer Relationship Management platforms centralize customer data, interactions, and history to improve sales efficiency and customer support.

Practical Example

“HubSpot, Salesforce, and Pipedrive enable lead tracking, opportunity conversion, and segmented email campaigns.”

Learn More

  • Choosing the Right CRM for SaaS
  • Integrating CRM with Stripe & WhatsApp

WhatsApp API

Official WhatsApp Business API enables programmatic sending/receiving of messages with verified delivery rates, media support, templates, and automations.

Practical Example

“Order notifications, appointment reminders, and automated support via WhatsApp bot integrated with your backend.”

Learn More

  • Setting Up WhatsApp API for Business
  • Marketing Automations with WhatsApp

Technical SEO

Technical SEO optimizes site structure (crawlability, indexing, speed, mobile-first, schema markup) to improve organic search rankings.

Practical Example

“Optimizing Core Web Vitals, implementing Structured Data, fixing canonical tags, and improving URL architecture.”

Learn More

  • Technical SEO Checklist 2025
  • Improving Core Web Vitals in Next.js

Stripe

Global payments platform offering APIs for card processing, recurring billing, marketplaces, and subscription management.

Practical Example

“Monthly subscriptions with Stripe Billing, one-time payments via Checkout, and split payments for marketplaces using Connect.”

Learn More

  • Stripe Billing: Scalable Subscriptions
  • Stripe Webhooks & Security

Neon

Serverless PostgreSQL with automatic branching per deploy and instant scaling, perfect for modern apps and small teams.

Practical Example

“Using Neon with Prisma ORM in a Next.js app to get automatic staging environments per PR and zero-downtime migrations.”

Learn More

  • Neon + Prisma: Complete Setup
  • Serverless PostgreSQL for Startups

CI/CD

Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery automate code building, testing, and deployment to ensure quality and frequent releases.

Practical Example

“GitHub Actions pipeline that runs tests, builds, and deploys to Vercel on every push to main, with preview deployments for each PR.”

Learn More

  • CI/CD with GitHub Actions
  • Automated Deploys on Vercel
